rulings.Case Title: EVELYN CASTELLANOS ET AL VS. KIM HONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 28, 2020, line 1. DEFENDANT 2236 SACRAMENTO STREET PARTNERS, LP, SF CABO, LLC, S&G CAPITAL ADVISORS, LLC, URBAN VALUE INVESTMENTS LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. There are triable issues of fact as to the indemnity re: Plaintiffs' claims against the moving cross-defendants based on the actions/inactions after December 5, 2017. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Motion as to the 41 out of 43 affirmative defenses is not supported by Points and Authorities. Triable issues of fact exist as to the affirmative defenses of Privity and Illegality. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: HOMERO ROCHAN ET AL VS. JEFF MOSLEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 28, 2020, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT HOMERO ROCHAN, DORI ROCHAN, ROMERO ROCHAN MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Special Motion to Strike Portions of Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden to show that the claims for slander of title and conspiracy arise our of protected activity. Cross-Complaint seeks relief expressly based on publications made "prior to filing of the within action" and not on the recordation of the lis pendens, which is recited in the Factual Allegations only along with 11 other paragraphs outlining the events preceding the filing of this action from 2008 to 2019. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BOBRIE ANN BROWN VS. AMILIA OTIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 28, 2020, line 6. DEFENDANT AMILIA OTIS DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTIANED without leave to amend. The operative Notice is fatally defective. CCP 1161(2). The Court notes that in addition to this fatal defect, First Amended Complaint fails to comply with CCP 1166 and purports to be based on two notices to pay rent or quit which were served and expired at different times. By service of a consecutive notice demanding rent Plaintiff created a tenancy even if original notice was ever successful in terminating a tenancy. Original notice cannot support a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on the facts alleged in the First Amended Complaint/Declaration in support thereto. =(501/CFH)
